interface: Jira Cloud platform REST API v3 enhanced JQL search (POST /rest/api/3/search/jql)

test_credentials.api_token: dummy-jira-api-token-5860 (dummy; must never appear in error text)

body.jql: a bounded JQL expression (a search restriction is required)
body.maxResults: page size; documented default 50; the server may return fewer items per page
body.fields: JSON array of field names — the default is id only, so wanted fields must be listed explicitly
body.expand: comma-delimited STRING (e.g. "names,schema"), omitted entirely when unused
body.nextPageToken: omitted on the first page; on later pages, exactly the token from the previous response

search.legacy_rule: the old /rest/api/3/search startAt/total interface was removed — never call it and never send startAt

pagination.nextPageToken: opaque continuation token in the response; present while more pages exist, absent/null on the last page; expires in 7 days; never rebuild or decode it
pagination.empty_intermediate_pages: a page may carry issues: [] together with a nextPageToken — the exporter must keep following the token, not stop on the empty page
pagination.short_pages: a page may hold fewer than maxResults issues even when more pages follow

issues.typed_values: field values are JSON-typed and must be preserved exactly: ints stay ints, floats stay floats, null stays null, objects (status, resolution) keep their nested shape, arrays (labels) stay arrays, unicode strings survive

errors.invalid_search: 400 for an invalid/unbounded JQL query or an invalid or expired nextPageToken
errors.redaction: neither the API token, the email, nor the base64 auth blob may appear in error text

test_fixture_notes.export_file: issues_export.jsonl — one JSON object per line: {id, key, fields}
test_fixture_notes.page_script: page 1: 1 issue + token PT-1; page 2: 0 issues + token PT-2 (legal); page 3: 2 issues, no token
